title: Smoky Saudi-Arabian Rice Pilaf
description: A hearty one-pot dish featuring smoky flavors and gooey rice, reminiscent of traditional Saudi-Arabian cuisine.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and red bell pepper, cooking until the vegetables are softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, cooking for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the rice, chicken broth, and water to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the pot.
Simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in the labneh cheese, until melted and gooey.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish and serve hot.
Serve the pilaf garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.

Note: Traditionally, this dish is cooked over a wood fire or on a stovetop with a smoky wood chip, which adds a smoky flavor to the dish. To replicate this, you can add a few drops of liquid smoke to the pot during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
If you want a crispy, caramelized crust on the bottom of the pilaf, cook it for an additional 10-15 minutes, uncovered.
If you prefer a creamier pilaf, you can add 1/4 cup of heavy cream or yogurt towards the end of cooking.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
